expectations.text: Checks if sprint-status.yaml exists before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'Check if _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml exists.' This is the very first action, per the SKILL.md Startup Sequence. The plan confirms: 'The file does NOT exist.'

expectations.text: Invokes bmad-help skill when sprint-status.yaml is missing
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'Invoke skill: "bmad-help"' and 'Report the suggestions returned by bmad-help to the user.' The detailed execution trace confirms: '3. Action: Invoke skill "bmad-help" for next-action suggestions.'

expectations.text: Does NOT create a team or spawn sub-agents
expectations.evidence: Step 3 titled 'There Is No Step 3': 'The orchestrator halts after step 2. No team is created, no sub-agents are spawned, no stories are processed.' Summary table confirms: 'Sub-agents spawned: None' and 'Team created: No.'

claims.claim: sprint-status.yaml is the gate that confirms the project has completed prior BMAD phases
claims.evidence: Plan explains: 'The existence of sprint-status.yaml is the gate that confirms the project has completed prior BMAD phases (requirements, architecture, planning) and has entered implementation.'

claims.claim: bmad-help would guide the user on what specific BMAD commands to run
claims.evidence: This is a reasonable inference but cannot be verified from the execution plan alone -- the plan does not show the actual output of bmad-help.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted for this scenario. They test the gating behavior, the fallback to bmad-help, and the guard against premature action. All three are discriminating.
